description: Capitalisation des apprentissages au fil du développement. Chaque entrée est créée après une story et consultée avant la suivante pour éviter de reproduire les erreurs et réutiliser les bonnes pratiques.

categories.pitfall: Piège rencontré — à éviter dans les prochaines stories
categories.fix: Bug ou erreur corrigée — retenir la solution
categories.design: Subtilité design (maquette) à ne pas oublier
categories.refactor: Refactoring à faire ou dette technique identifiée

lessons.title: pydantic-settings is a separate package
lessons.what: config.py imports from pydantic_settings which is NOT included in pydantic. Build fails if pydantic-settings is missing from pyproject.toml dependencies.
lessons.rule: Always add pydantic-settings>=2.6.0 as a dependency when using BaseSettings from pydantic_settings.

lessons.title: Hatchling needs explicit package path
lessons.what: Hatchling build backend expects a directory matching the project name (aura_wealth_backend). Since our package is named 'app', we must declare [tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel] packages = ['app'].
lessons.rule: When the Python package directory differs from pyproject.toml project name, always add [tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel] packages = ['app'] to pyproject.toml.

lessons.title: System Python version mismatch — use venv
lessons.what: macOS has python3 (3.14) and pip (3.11) pointing to different Pythons. PEP 668 blocks system-level installs. Always use a venv for backend dev.
lessons.rule: Always create a venv (python3 -m venv .venv) in /backend before installing deps. Document this in CLAUDE.md commands.

lessons.title: All maquette CSS classes replicated in index.css
lessons.what: Faithfully copied every CSS class from aura-maquette.html into frontend/src/index.css (glass-panel, card-hover, glow-line, progress-bar, tag, spark-ai, stagger, tab-indicator, tooltip, scrollbar, animations). This is the single source of truth for custom CSS.
lessons.rule: When adding new CSS from the maquette, always add it to index.css — never inline. Keep Tailwind for utilities, index.css for maquette-specific classes.

lessons.title: Tab navigation via Context, not React Router
lessons.what: The maquette uses tab-based SPA navigation (no URL changes). Using React Router for tab switching adds unnecessary complexity. NavigationContext with useState is simpler and matches the maquette's behavior.
lessons.rule: Use NavigationContext for tab switching. Keep react-router-dom as a dependency for potential deep-linking later but don't import it in main.tsx until needed.

lessons.title: Nav-btn --active-tab CSS variable must be set per button
lessons.what: The ::after pseudo-element on nav-btn uses var(--active-tab) for its color. This CSS variable must be set via inline style on the active button to match the tab-specific color (gold for home, green for epargne, etc.).
lessons.rule: When rendering nav buttons, set --active-tab inline style on the active button so the underline color matches the tab identity.

lessons.title: Three.js bundle is 615KB — needs lazy import
lessons.what: Three.js adds ~450KB to the main JS chunk, triggering Vite's 500KB warning. For production, ParticleBackground should be lazy-loaded with React.lazy() + Suspense.
lessons.rule: When Three.js chunk size becomes a concern, use dynamic import: const ParticleBackground = lazy(() => import('@/components/webgl/ParticleBackground')). Not blocking for MVP but must be done before release.

lessons.title: WebGL cleanup is critical in React strict mode
lessons.what: React.StrictMode double-mounts in dev. The useEffect cleanup must dispose renderer, geometry, material, and remove the canvas DOM node. Without cleanup, you get duplicate canvases.
lessons.rule: Always return a cleanup function from WebGL useEffect that calls renderer.dispose(), geo.dispose(), mat.dispose(), cancelAnimationFrame(), and removes event listeners.

lessons.title: Dashboard components follow a strict template
lessons.what: All dashboard blocks follow the same structure: glass-panel p-6 rounded-2xl card-hover, title with icon (font-display text-lg font-light, border-b border-gray-800 pb-2), SparkAi absolute top-4 right-4, data rows in glass-panel-inner p-3 rounded-lg.
lessons.rule: When creating new blocks for other tabs (Epargne, Bourse, Immo, PE), follow this exact template. The title icon color changes per tab (gold=home, green=epargne, blue=bourse, amber=immo, purple=pe).

lessons.title: Tooltip in segmented bar needs tooltip-wrapper CSS class
lessons.what: The distribution bar segments use tooltip-wrapper class from index.css for hover tooltips. The tooltip must be a child span with class tooltip-text. This pattern will be reused for other segmented bars.
lessons.rule: Always use the tooltip-wrapper/tooltip-text CSS pattern from index.css for hover tooltips. Don't use a JS-based tooltip for simple hover reveals.

lessons.title: Unused imports with strict tsconfig
lessons.description: noUnusedLocals causes build failure for unused imports (e.g. useState imported but not used). Always verify imports match actual usage before building.

lessons.title: Batch component creation scales well
lessons.description: Creating 7+ components in one batch (all Immo + PE views) then verifying build once is efficient. Pattern: create all files → single build → fix → done.

lessons.title: Context provider for AI panel
lessons.description: Using React Context for AI panel state (AiContext) allows any SparkAi button to trigger contextual messages without prop drilling. Pattern reusable for any global panel.

lessons.title: Read files before editing
lessons.description: The Edit tool requires Read first. When batch-editing many files, read all target files in parallel before applying edits to avoid tool errors.

lessons.title: passlib incompatible with bcrypt>=5.0
lessons.what: passlib 1.7.4 crashes with bcrypt 5.x due to removed __about__ attribute and changed API. The detect_wrap_bug function fails with ValueError on password length.
lessons.rule: Use bcrypt directly (bcrypt.hashpw / bcrypt.checkpw) instead of passlib. Drop passlib from dependencies entirely. This is simpler and avoids the abandoned passlib library.

lessons.title: Pydantic EmailStr needs email-validator pkg
lessons.what: Using pydantic.EmailStr in models requires the email-validator package. Pydantic does not include it by default — must add pydantic[email] to dependencies.
lessons.rule: When using EmailStr in Pydantic models, declare dependency as pydantic[email]>=2.10.0 in pyproject.toml, not just pydantic>=2.10.0.

lessons.title: JWT auth with graceful mock fallback
lessons.what: get_current_user checks is_mongo_available(). When MongoDB is down (dev mode), it returns MOCK_USER without requiring auth. When MongoDB is up, it requires a valid JWT Bearer token.
lessons.rule: Always maintain the mock fallback in get_current_user so devs can work without MongoDB. The auth endpoints (register/login/refresh) return 503 without DB — this is correct.

lessons.title: patrimoine_data stores domain data in 'data' field
lessons.what: Multi-tenant migration moves from 5 separate collections to one 'patrimoine_data' collection. Each doc has {user_id, domain, data: {...}, updated_at}. Routers must read doc['data'], not doc directly.
lessons.rule: When querying patrimoine_data, always access doc['data'] for the domain payload. The doc itself has envelope fields (user_id, domain, updated_at) that are not part of the domain model.

lessons.title: AuthGate pattern replaces React Router for auth
lessons.what: Instead of ProtectedRoute + React Router, we use an AuthGate component in App.tsx that conditionally renders LoginPage/RegisterPage or the authenticated dashboard. This avoids adding react-router-dom complexity since we use tab navigation.
lessons.rule: Use AuthGate component (isAuthenticated ? dashboard : login) instead of ProtectedRoute + router. Tab navigation via NavigationContext doesn't need URL-based routing for auth.

lessons.title: Module-level vars trigger TS6133 if only written
lessons.what: TypeScript's noUnusedLocals treats module-level 'let' variables as unused if they're only assigned but never read. _refreshToken was set in login/logout but never read, causing TS error.
lessons.rule: If a module-level variable is write-only for now, export a getter function so TS sees it as 'read'. This also prepares the API for future consumers.

lessons.title: CRUD via services/patrimoine.py, never direct DB writes
lessons.what: All domain write operations (PUT/PATCH/reset) go through services/patrimoine.py which handles deep merge, audit logging, and upsert. Routers are thin — they validate auth and delegate.
lessons.rule: Routers NEVER call db.patrimoine_data.update_one directly. Always use put_domain_data/patch_domain_data/reset_domain_data from services/patrimoine.py. This ensures audit trail consistency.

lessons.title: Write endpoints require role check, GET does not
lessons.what: GET endpoints use Depends(get_current_user) — any authenticated user can read. PUT/PATCH/reset use Depends(require_role(abonne, super_admin)) — visiteurs cannot write.
lessons.rule: All write operations (PUT, PATCH, POST reset, DELETE) must use require_role(). GET endpoints only need get_current_user.

lessons.title: InlineEdit + ConfirmDialog pattern for inline editing
lessons.what: InlineEdit wraps value display with hover pencil → click input → Enter/blur → ConfirmDialog (old vs new) → onSave callback. The ConfirmDialog is rendered inside InlineEdit using pendingValue state. RequireRole fallback renders read-only span for visiteurs.
lessons.rule: When adding inline editing to any block: use InlineEdit with label prop, format function, and field path. Pass onPatch from parent view via useMutation. InlineEdit handles ConfirmDialog internally.

lessons.title: PATCH body needs _unflatten_dict for dot-notation keys
lessons.what: Frontend sends dot-notation keys like {'fiscal.ir': 13000}. The _deep_merge function expects nested dicts. Added _unflatten_dict to convert dot-notation to nested before merging.
lessons.rule: When frontend sends PATCH with dot-notation field paths, always run _unflatten_dict(patch) before _deep_merge. The audit log still uses _flatten_dict for individual field logging.

lessons.title: useApiData refetch via tick state
lessons.what: Added refetch() to useApiData by introducing a tick counter as useEffect dependency. Calling refetch increments tick, triggering re-fetch without resetting data (preserves prev state during loading).
lessons.rule: Use refetch() from useApiData after successful mutations to reload data. The pattern: mutate → toast → refetch. DashboardView passes handlePatch callback to child blocks.

lessons.title: Array CRUD via __append_ / __delete_ prefixed PATCH keys
lessons.what: Frontend sends __append_livrets: {item} to add, __delete_livrets: index to remove, and livrets.{index}: {item} to update an array element. Backend _apply_array_ops extracts these before deep merge.
lessons.rule: For array mutations, use __append_<field> (append item), __delete_<field> (delete by index), or <field>.<index> (update by index) in PATCH body. Backend handles these in patrimoine.py _apply_array_ops + _deep_merge_with_arrays.

lessons.title: Modal form pattern: parent passes livret + index, form manages state
lessons.what: EpargneView holds editingLivret/editingIndex state. LivretBlock calls onEdit(livret, index) on click. LivretForm receives livret prop to pre-fill, livretIndex for PATCH targeting. onSaved triggers refetch.
lessons.rule: For modal CRUD forms: parent view owns open/editing state, block component calls onEdit(item, index), form component handles validation/API/toast. onSaved callback triggers parent refetch.
